subject: first-run.md accuracy review
body:
# `docs/getting-started/first-run.md` — Accuracy Report

Reviewed on 2026-05-26 against `D:\Drive\Source\SquadDash-public\SquadDash\`.

---

## Area 1 — First-Launch Flow

**⚠ Partially accurate**

The doc presents Node.js verification and Squad installation as an **automatic first-launch sequence**. The code tells a different story:

| Doc claim | Reality |
|---|---|
| "SquadDash verifies node/npm/npx on first launch" | The check (`SquadInstallerService.ValidatePrerequisitesAsync`) **only runs when the user clicks the "Install Squad" button** — not automatically on startup |
| "Installs Squad CLI via `npm install`" | Command is actually `npm install --save-dev @bradygaster/squad-cli` (`SquadCliCommands.cs:5`) |
| "Runs `squad init`" | Command is actually `npx @bradygaster/squad-cli init` (`SquadCliCommands.cs:8`) |
| Creates `.squad/team.md`, `.squad/routing.md`, `.squad/config.json` | The C# code only checks `team.md` as the workspace-init signal (`SquadInstallerService.cs:374-376`). `config.json` appears nowhere in installation-state logic. |

`App.xaml.cs:OnStartup` does nothing beyond reading workspace paths, resolving a startup folder, and showing `MainWindow`. There is no Node.js auto-check at startup.

---

## Area 2 — Workspace Selection Flow (doc lines 35–43)

**❌ Inaccurate**

The doc implies that steps 1–4 ("What SquadDash Does") execute automatically when the user **opens a workspace folder**:

> 1. Checks if `package.json` exists (creates one if not)
> 2. Installs the Squad CLI locally via `npm install`
> 3. Applies Windows compatibility fixes to the installed CLI
> 4. Runs `squad init` if `.squad/team.md` doesn't exist

**None of those steps are automatic.** They are performed inside `SquadInstallerService.InstallAsync`, called only from `InstallSquadButton_Click` (`MainWindow.xaml.cs:11420`). Opening a workspace merely shows the "Squad is not installed" banner and an **Install Squad** button. The user must click it to trigger any of those steps.

What does happen automatically on workspace open:
- `SquadInstallationStateService` reads disk state (`team.md` / `package.json` / `node_modules/.bin/squad.cmd`) to decide whether to show the Install banner.
- If Squad is already installed, agent cards are loaded from `team.md` directly.

---

## Area 3 — Voice Input Setup (doc lines 140–145)

**⚠ Partially accurate**

The doc says: *"Enter your Azure Cognitive Services Speech key and region"*

What actually exists in `PreferencesWindow.cs`:

| Field / Control | Present? |
|---|---|
| Azure Speech API Key field | ✅ Yes (`_apiKeyPasswordBox`, line 160) |
| Azure Speech Region field | ✅ Yes (`_speechRegionBox`, line 178) |
| **OpenAI Whisper** radio + API key field | ✅ Yes (`_openAiSpeechRadio`, `_openAiSpeechKeyPasswordBox`, lines 210-251) — **not mentioned in the doc** |
| Microphone device picker | ❌ Not present |
| Language picker | ✅ Yes (`_speechLanguageComboBox`, line 187) — **not mentioned in the doc** |

The Preferences Speech page is a **provider choice** (Azure Cognitive Services vs. OpenAI Whisper), each with its own API key. The doc omits the Whisper alternative entirely.

Double-Ctrl PTT: **✅ Accurate** (`CtrlDoubleTapGestureTracker`, `PreferencesWindow.cs:82`).

---

## Area 4 — Launch Command (doc line 18)

**⚠ Partially accurate**

Doc says: `dotnet run --project SquadDash`

- `SquadDash.csproj` `OutputType` is `WinExe` (line 4) — `dotnet run` works for WPF projects in development, so the command itself is not wrong.
- The csproj defines a `SquadDashLauncher` project (`SquadDash.csproj:16`) and a `BuildStableLauncherAndDeploy` MSBuild target that deploys build output into versioned run-slots (`SquadDash.csproj:114-117`).
- The assembly name is `SquadDash.App` (csproj line 9), not `SquadDash`. The compiled binary is `SquadDash.App.exe`. The launcher produces `SquadDash.exe` as the stable entry point via slot deployment.
- Running `dotnet run --project SquadDash` **bypasses the launcher entirely** and skips slot deployment. For development this is acceptable, but the doc does not mention the launcher at all.

---

## Recommended Fixes — Specific Line Changes

| # | Line(s) | What to change |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Lines 27–32 (Node.js check) | Clarify the check is part of the **Install Squad** flow, not an automatic startup check. Reword: *"When you click Install Squad, SquadDash first verifies that `node`, `npm`, and `npx` are accessible on your PATH…"* |
| 2 | Lines 35–43 (Workspace Selection, "What SquadDash Does") | Remove the implication that these steps run automatically on folder open. Add: *"These steps run when you click the **Install Squad** button that appears in the top banner when Squad is not yet installed in the workspace."* |
| 3 | Line 41 | Change `npm install` → `npm install --save-dev @bradygaster/squad-cli` |
| 4 | Lines 43 and 56 | Change `squad init` → `npx @bradygaster/squad-cli init` |
| 5 | Lines 59–61 | Qualify `.squad/config.json` or remove it — C# installation-state code does not create or check this file. Verify with Squad CLI maintainers before documenting it. |
| 6 | Lines 140–145 (Voice Input) | Replace *"Enter your Azure Cognitive Services Speech key and region"* with: *"Choose a speech provider (Azure Cognitive Services or OpenAI Whisper) and enter the corresponding API key. For Azure, also enter the region (e.g. `eastus`). A language picker is also available."* |
| 7 | Line 18 (launch command) | Add a note: *"This runs the main WPF app directly (development mode). The full launcher experience — including hot-restart and slot management — uses the `SquadDashLauncher` project or the installed `SquadDash.exe` binary."* |

actions.label: Fix the inaccuracies
actions.prompt:
Arjun: Argus Weld reviewed `docs/getting-started/first-run.md` for accuracy on 2026-05-26 and found the following inaccuracies. Please update the doc to match actual codebase behaviour. Do NOT change any C# code. Build is not required — this is a docs-only fix. Commit to main with message: `docs: correct first-run.md inaccuracies found in accuracy audit`.

---

## File to edit
`docs/getting-started/first-run.md`

## Finding 1 — Node.js check is NOT automatic at startup (lines 27–32)

Doc currently says:
> SquadDash verifies that `node`, `npm`, and `npx` are accessible on your `PATH`. If any are missing, you'll see an error dialog.

Reality: The check lives in `SquadInstallerService.ValidatePrerequisitesAsync` and only runs when the user clicks the **Install Squad** button. `App.xaml.cs:OnStartup` has no Node.js check at all.

**Fix:** Change the wording to clarify this check is part of the Install Squad flow, not an automatic startup check:
> When you click **Install Squad**, SquadDash first verifies that `node`, `npm`, and `npx` are accessible on your `PATH`. If any are missing, you'll see an error message in the install panel.

---

## Finding 2 — "What SquadDash Does" steps are user-triggered, not automatic (lines 39–43)

Doc currently implies that the four steps (check package.json, install CLI, apply compat fixes, run init) happen automatically when a workspace folder is opened.

Reality: All four steps are performed only inside `SquadInstallerService.InstallAsync`, which is called exclusively from `InstallSquadButton_Click` in `MainWindow.xaml.cs:11420`. Opening a workspace only reads disk state and shows the "Squad is not installed" banner. The user must click **Install Squad** to trigger these steps.

**Fix:** Add a sentence before the numbered list, such as:
> When you click **Install Squad** in the top banner, SquadDash performs the following steps:

---

## Finding 3 — npm install command is more specific (line 41)

Doc says: `npm install`
Actual command (`SquadCliCommands.cs:5`): `npm install --save-dev @bradygaster/squad-cli`

**Fix:** Change line 41 to:
> 2. Installs the Squad CLI locally via `npm install --save-dev @bradygaster/squad-cli`

---

## Finding 4 — squad init command is different (lines 43 and 56)

Doc says: `squad init`
Actual command (`SquadCliCommands.cs:8`): `npx @bradygaster/squad-cli init`

**Fix:** Update both occurrences:
- Line 43: `Runs \`squad init\`` → `Runs \`npx @bradygaster/squad-cli init\``
- Line 56 code block: change `squad init` → `npx @bradygaster/squad-cli init`

---

## Finding 5 — .squad/config.json not verified (lines 59–61)

Doc says `squad init` creates `.squad/config.json`. The C# installation-state code (`SquadInstallationStateService.cs`) only checks for `.squad/team.md` as the workspace-init signal. `config.json` appears nowhere in any C# installation logic.

**Fix:** Remove `.squad/config.json` from the bullet list, or add a qualifier: *"(depending on Squad CLI version)"*. Safe conservative fix — just remove it unless confirmed:
```markdown
This creates:
- `.squad/team.md` — Your AI team roster
- `.squad/routing.md` — Routing rules for work assignment
```

---

## Finding 6 — Voice Input section only mentions Azure, omits OpenAI Whisper (lines 140–145)

Doc says:
> 2. Enter your **Azure Cognitive Services Speech** key and region

Reality (`PreferencesWindow.cs:200-251`): The Speech preferences page has a **provider radio button** offering:
- Azure Cognitive Services (API key + region fields)
- OpenAI Whisper (separate API key field)

There is also a **language picker** (`_speechLanguageComboBox`) not mentioned in the doc.

**Fix:** Replace step 2 with:
> 2. Choose a speech provider:
>    - **Azure Cognitive Services** — enter your Speech API key and region (e.g. `eastus`)
>    - **OpenAI Whisper** — enter your OpenAI API key
>    Optionally select a recognition language from the language picker.

---

## Finding 7 — Launch command omits SquadDashLauncher (line 18)

Doc says: `dotnet run --project SquadDash`

`dotnet run` works for development. However, `SquadDash.csproj` has a `BuildStableLauncherAndDeploy` target that deploys build output through a `SquadDashLauncher` project, which manages hot-restart and versioned run-slots. The assembly name is `SquadDash.App` (not `SquadDash`), and the installed binary is `SquadDash.exe` produced by the launcher.

**Fix:** Add a note after the `dotnet run` code block:
> This runs the main WPF project directly (suitable for development). The app ships via the `SquadDashLauncher` project, which handles hot-restart and slot management. To use the full launch pipeline, run the `SquadDashLauncher` project or use the installed `SquadDash.exe`.
